behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: three source clients -> aggregation/composition -> summary input payload. MOCK-MISS: this is not single-function logic; it appears when modules are wired. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required to catch omission/overwrite bugs; in-process fakes for the three source adapters are sufficient. LLM-DEP: behavior checks input to summary step, not model output quality. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks: (a) no, (b) no, (c) no, (d) no.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline with stubbed source adapters returning distinct sentinel fields from metrics, issues, and deploy logs; intercept the payload handed to the summary generator and assert all three sections are present and correctly mapped.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is error propagation across modules (source client failure -> section fallback marker -> report still rendered). MOCK-MISS: this requires interaction between fetch/orchestration/render layers, not a lone function. REAL-INFRA: unavailability can be represented by adapter errors/timeouts in-process; real network is not required for this behavior. LLM-DEP: correctness is fallback control flow, not model judgment. Rejection gate for System: behavior text requires graceful degradation semantics, not real transport characteristics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real orchestrator + renderer with one source adapter configured to raise an unavailability error and two normal adapters; assert report generation completes and includes `data unavailable` only for the failed section.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is summary content quality. Per LLM-DEP, if correctness depends on what the real summarizer produces, stubs hide hallucination/omission/paraphrase errors. This behavior is about semantic faithfulness of generated text to input values, which is model-output dependent. Rejection gate for Workflow: behavior describes a single summary-generation decision, not multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute summary generation against the real model on a curated set of input fixtures with known key values; score outputs for factual inclusion/consistency (e.g., required values present, no contradictory numbers) over repeated runs with pass-rate thresholds.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real external delivery and inbox reception, which depends on real HTTP/SMTP behavior and end-to-end wiring. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE: mocked mail clients can pass while real auth/routing/provider behavior fails. Rejection gate for Agentic: behavior text is inbox delivery correctness; it does not require judging summary quality, so model-output validation is not the target boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run the script end-to-end in an environment hitting real source endpoints (or realistic test endpoints) and a real email delivery path to a test inbox; verify one delivered message to configured recipients with expected report structure/content sections.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary spans real delivery failure mode plus persistence and cross-run retry behavior. REAL-INFRA: unreachable service and retry semantics can differ with real transport failures; filesystem persistence across runs is part of boundary. MOCK-HIDE: constant mocked exceptions may miss real client error classes/timing/state transitions. Rejection gate for Workflow: this is retry/state recovery logic, not multi-agent model decision chaining.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real delivery unreachability in a test environment (e.g., blocked mail endpoint), execute one run and assert fallback report file is written; execute the next scheduled run with service restored and assert queued report is retried and delivered, then fallback file/state is cleared.
